---
title: "Quickstart"
description: "Start building custom sandbox templates with the Go SDK."
---

# Quickstart

E2B templates let you define custom sandboxes in Go. You can choose the base image, environment variables, copied files, build commands, and a [start command](/docs/template/start-ready-command) that is captured into the resulting snapshot.

This gives you sandboxes that can start with your filesystem and long-running processes already prepared.

There are two ways to get started:

- use the E2B CLI
- create the template manually with the Go SDK

## CLI

You can use the E2B CLI to initialize a new template project.

1. Install the latest E2B CLI.
2. Run `e2b template init`.
3. Follow the prompts.
4. Inspect the generated files and `README.md`.

## Manual

### Install the packages

Inside your Go module:

```bash
go get github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk
go get github.com/joho/godotenv
```

Create a `.env` file:

```bash
E2B_API_KEY=e2b_***
```

### Create a template file

Create `template.go`:

```go
package main

import (
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

var template = e2b.Template(nil).
	FromBaseImage().
	SetEnvs(map[string]string{
		"HELLO": "Hello, World!",
	}).
	SetStartCmd("echo $HELLO", e2b.WaitForTimeout(5_000))
```

### Create a development build script

Create `build_dev.go`:

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"log"

	"github.com/joho/godotenv"
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	_ = godotenv.Load()

	_, err := e2b.Build(context.Background(), template, "template-tag-dev", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			CpuCount:    1,
			MemoryMB:    1024,
			OnBuildLogs: e2b.DefaultBuildLogger(),
		},
	})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

### Create a production build script

Create `build_prod.go`:

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"log"

	"github.com/joho/godotenv"
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	_ = godotenv.Load()

	_, err := e2b.Build(context.Background(), template, "template-tag", &e2b.BuildOptions{
		BasicBuildOptions: e2b.BasicBuildOptions{
			CpuCount:    1,
			MemoryMB:    1024,
			OnBuildLogs: e2b.DefaultBuildLogger(),
		},
	})
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
}
```

### Build the template

Build the development template:

```bash
go run build_dev.go template.go
```

Build the production template:

```bash
go run build_prod.go template.go
```

## Create a sandbox from the template

Create `create_sandbox.go`:

```go
package main

import (
	"context"
	"log"

	"github.com/joho/godotenv"
	e2b "github.com/superduck-ai/e2b-go-sdk"
)

func main() {
	_ = godotenv.Load()
	ctx := context.Background()

	devSandbox, err := e2b.Create(ctx, "template-tag-dev", nil)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	prodSandbox, err := e2b.Create(ctx, "template-tag", nil)
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	_ = devSandbox
	_ = prodSandbox
}
```

The template name is the identifier you use later with `Create(...)`.

For more detail on builder methods, see [Defining Template](/docs/template/defining-template). For build status and logs, see [Build](/docs/template/build) and [Logging](/docs/template/logging).
